exact.backward_induction: Q_N>0 on the interval and Q_n(-100)>0 imply successively Q_(N-1)>0,...,Q_0>0
exact.conditional_forward_theorem: uniform eventual order-four positivity on [-100,0] implies H_(4,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=0 and -100<=lambda<=0
exact.endpoint_warning: eventual positivity at lambda=0 alone does not provide the uniform interior tail hypothesis
exact.finite_confinement: uniform eventual positivity confines every possible first crossing to the finite set 0<=n<N
exact.lambda_zero_consequence: uniform eventual order-four positivity on [-100,0] implies H_(4,n)(0)>0 for every n>=0
exact.uniform_asymptotic_target: prove one N works simultaneously for every lambda in [-100,0]
exact.uniform_tail_hypothesis: exists N such that Q_n(lambda)>0 for every n>=N and -100<=lambda<=0

proof_boundary: This artifact proves that a uniform eventual positive order-four tail on [-100,0] is sufficient for full contiguous order-four forward invariance, without a global coefficient ceiling. It does not prove that uniform tail, unconditional order-four invariance, arbitrary-column order four, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.
remaining_target: exists N such that Q_n(lambda)>0 for every n>=N and -100<=lambda<=0

rows.claim: Every contiguous order-four determinant is strictly positive at the initial heat parameter.
rows.proof_boundary: Previously certified all-shift entry at lambda=-100.

rows.claim: The completed order-three layer makes the order-four flow one-sided cooperative throughout the target heat interval.
rows.proof_boundary: Contiguous order-four flow only; the spatial tail is separate.

rows.claim: Prove that one eventual order-four tail is positive uniformly across the compact heat interval.

diagnostics.warning: eventual positivity at lambda=0 alone does not provide the uniform interior tail hypothesis

rows.formula: exists N such that Q_n(lambda)>0 for every n>=N and -100<=lambda<=0
rows.proof_boundary: Open uniform asymptotic theorem; endpoint eventual positivity is insufficient.

rows.claim: A uniform positive tail confines any possible loss of positivity to finitely many order-four coordinates.
rows.formula: uniform eventual positivity confines every possible first crossing to the finite set 0<=n<N
rows.proof_boundary: Exact consequence of the open uniform-tail hypothesis.

rows.claim: Each finite coordinate has an exact positive integrating-factor representation driven only by its next neighbor.
Starting at the uniform positive tail boundary, variation of constants propagates strict positivity backward through every remaining finite index.
rows.formula: Q_N>0 on the interval and Q_n(-100)>0 imply successively Q_(N-1)>0,...,Q_0>0
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional only on co4etfi_03; no coefficient ceiling is required.

rows.claim: Uniform eventual positivity closes contiguous order-four forward invariance from lambda=-100 through lambda=0.
rows.formula: uniform eventual order-four positivity on [-100,0] implies H_(4,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=0 and -100<=lambda<=0
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional on the single uniform-tail theorem; contiguous order four only.

rows.claim: The same hypothesis promotes the existing finite lambda-zero prefix to an all-shift contiguous order-four theorem.
rows.formula: uniform eventual order-four positivity on [-100,0] implies H_(4,n)(0)>0 for every n>=0
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional; not arbitrary-column order four, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.
